note: Targeted PR Freelance scrape, 7-day window (2026-04-28 → 2026-05-05) plus funding-driven intent signals back to 2026-04-15. Three credible new openings cleared the 55+ threshold. Deduped against pr-freelance batches from 2026-05-04 (Offchain Labs, GlossGenius, Upstart), 2026-05-01 (Siege Media, Mission North, BVNK, Coinbase Base, Atticus, Cloudbeds, FactSet, Robinhood), 2026-04-30 (5WPR, HockeyStack, Crypto.com, Courier Health, Forward Financing), 2026-04-27 targeted (SerpApi, PayNearMe, Paddle, Loti AI), and pr-freelance leads inside daily scrapes through 2026-05-04. Marquee discovery is Rain — yesterday's Mastercard principal-member announcement plus $250M Series C in Jan creates the strongest post-funding comms-build signal of the month. Stablecoin/payments PR is the hottest sub-sector — three of three leads sit in stablecoin/crypto-payments/payment-network space, continuing the BVNK/Coinbase Base/Robinhood Crypto pattern. Crypto fluency is now the gating skill for the most actionable leads.

leads.name: Rain
leads.industry: Enterprise stablecoin payments infrastructure — issues stablecoin cards and on-chain settlement rails for institutional customers (Western Union, Nuvei, KAST, +200 partners)
leads.fundingStage: $250M Series C Jan 2026 led by ICONIQ at $1.95B valuation; $338M total raised; backers Sapphire, Dragonfly, Bessemer, Galaxy Ventures, Lightspeed, Norwest, FirstMark, Endeavor Catalyst

contacts.name: Rain CEO / Founders
contacts.title: Direct LinkedIn target — verify exact CEO name; small enough team that 1-2 connection hops should land

contacts.name: Rain Head of Marketing / Comms
contacts.title: Comms-function owner during current Mastercard announcement cycle

contacts.name: Rain Incumbent PR Agency
contacts.title: Identify via Mastercard partnership press release and Series C announcement boilerplate — pitch on overflow

leads.notes: Combined 83 (A grade) — TOP LEAD of the week and strongest post-funding comms-build signal of the month. Rain (1) closed $250M Series C four months ago — Mark's signal #4 still in active window; (2) yesterday — May 4, 2026 — became Mastercard Principal Member and announced co-issued stablecoin cards. Tier-1 announcement cycle dropping into a function with marketing roles posted but no senior PR/comms role visible — capacity gap is real. Sector bullseye: stablecoins + cards + institutional payments + Mastercard rail. Pitch THIS WEEK — 5-day window before news cycle moves on. Two-track: (1) direct LinkedIn note to CEO + Head of Marketing offering 90-day fixed retainer for tier-1 media seeding (Bloomberg/WSJ/FT/The Block/Fortune) plus exec thought leadership and follow-on launch cycles, no logo credit; (2) parallel-pitch incumbent agency (identify via Mastercard partnership press boilerplate) on overflow capacity. Realistic retainer $15-25K/month for 60-90 days. CRITICAL RISK: same crypto fluency bar as BVNK/Coinbase Base/Tether/Offchain Labs — Mark must explain stablecoin reserves, on-chain settlement, card-network principal membership, and institutional-vs-retail crypto split in 5 minutes. If yes, push hard NOW. If no, skip — not a learn-on-the-job lead.

leads.name: Tether
leads.industry: Stablecoin issuer — USDT (largest stablecoin by market cap); expanding into AI/Web3 ecosystem (WDK, QVAC, Hadron, Keet, Holepunch)
leads.fundingStage: Profitable private company; multi-billion-dollar reserves; strategic ecosystem expansion underway

contacts.name: Tether CMO / Head of Communications
contacts.title: Direct LinkedIn target — search 'Tether communications' on LinkedIn to identify

leads.notes: Combined 76 (B grade). Tether is searching for a PR Manager (Product Communications) covering its product/AI ecosystem (WDK, QVAC, Hadron, Keet, Holepunch). JD calls for 5-8 years PR/product comms with crypto/AI/frontier tech exposure. Senior IC seat, NOT a Head of role — but it's a credible interim-during-search target: Tether's product-and-ecosystem narrative is sprawling and the role being open and posted at $112-144K full salary suggests current capacity gap. Sector is hottest part of Mark's Rolodex (payments/stablecoins/regulatory). Compensation tier confirms budget for senior PR work. 100% remote removes geographic friction. Pitch interim coverage during search, NOT candidacy: 'While you run the PR Manager search (12-16 weeks for crypto-fluent senior PR), I can carry product-launch comms and tier-1 media seeding across WDK/Hadron/Keet on a 60-90 day interim. Not a candidate, no logo credit, hand off cleanly.' Direct LinkedIn outreach to CMO/Head of Comms; Tether is famously inaccessible — warm intro through crypto-fintech press contact strongly preferred. Realistic retainer $12-18K/month for 60-90 days. RISK: Tether's institutional reputation is polarizing — some outlets won't take Tether-pitched stories on principle. Mark needs to be comfortable with that posture and have a Rolodex of editors who DO take Tether stories (The Block, CoinDesk, Bloomberg crypto desk, FT crypto desk). If Mark's PR reputation is built on conservative blue-chip clients only, brand mismatch.

leads.name: Capital One (Discover Network)
leads.industry: Financial services / payments — Capital One closed Discover acquisition 2025; building out Discover Network as global payments rail competing with Visa/Mastercard
leads.location: McLean, VA / Plano, TX / Richmond, VA (in-person expected; remote-flex unclear)
leads.fundingStage: Public company (NYSE: COF); Discover acquisition closed 2025

contacts.name: Capital One VP External Communications, Discover Network business unit
contacts.title: Hiring manager / sponsor for Director Corporate Communications, Global Payment Network — identify via LinkedIn search 'Capital One communications' + 'Discover Network'

leads.notes: Combined 72 (B grade). Posted April 3, 2026 — Director, Corporate Communications role specifically for Discover Network business unit. Calls for 9+ years PR/comms in financial services or technology — direct match for Mark's profile. Discover Network narrative is fresh multi-year post-acquisition story: building third major US payment rail, displacing share from Visa/Mastercard, courting issuer banks, regulatory framing of network competition. Exactly the multi-stakeholder financial-comms work Mark does. CATCH: Fortune 100 in-house seat, not fractional or freelance. Realistic angle is interim coverage if search stretches past 90 days OR specialized B2B/issuer-bank narrative workstream Capital One Enterprise Comms team isn't staffed for. Single shot, low-probability, worth ONE well-targeted email. Target VP External Communications (Discover Network business unit) via LinkedIn. Frame: 'Fractional senior coverage during search, specifically on issuer-bank acquisition narrative and regulatory framing of network competition. 90-day interim, no candidate conflict.' Probability of fractional engagement is low — Capital One has mature Enterprise Comms function and almost certainly agency of record (likely Edelman or Brunswick at this scale). But if search stretches past Q3 and there's an issuer-bank announcement cycle in window, door cracks open. Realistic retainer if it lands: $20-30K/month for 90 days; more likely specialized project engagement at $40-60K total. RISK: Mark must NOT pitch as competing with agency-of-record. Pitch as supplemental capacity for specific business-unit narrative too new/sub-vertical for corporate agency to cover deeply. No payment-network or issuer-bank reference story = skip.
